findings.description: The North Carolina state seal image/logo is missing from the HTML rendering
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 shows the North Carolina state seal at the top center of the letterhead. The HTML rendering does not display this image.

findings.description: The DEQ logo is missing from the HTML footer area
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 shows the DEQ logo at the bottom of the page next to the department address. The HTML rendering does not display this logo.

findings.description: Certified Mail and Return Receipt Requested underline formatting
findings.evidence: PDF shows 'Certified Mail' and 'Return Receipt Requested' with underline formatting. HTML preserves the underline formatting, so this is acceptable.

findings.description: The signature is missing from the HTML rendering on the final page
findings.evidence: PDF page 4 shows a handwritten signature by Judith A. Wehner. The HTML rendering does not include this signature image.

findings.description: The HTML has no h1 or any heading elements at all. The document lacks any heading structure — there are no h1, h2, h3, etc. tags used throughout the entire document. The letter's subject line (RE:) and numbered items could benefit from heading structure, but at minimum there should be a document title as h1.
findings.evidence:
The entire HTML uses only

,

    ,
  1. ,
    , and

findings.description: The first image has alt text 'North Carolina state seal' which is a reasonable description. The signature image has alt text 'Signature of Judith A. Wehner' which is accurate. However, two other images have empty alt text (alt=''), which is generic but not fabricated.
findings.evidence: Two tags have alt='' for what appear to be page break/background images.

findings.description: The page headers ('Certified Mail / Mr. Threatt / Page Two/Three/Four') are repeated both inline within the content flow and as structural dividers. This is acceptable since the PDF itself contains these repeated headers on each page. No substantive content duplication found.
findings.evidence: Page headers appear as <div class='page-header'> blocks matching PDF page headers.

findings.description: The HTML nests the page footer (department address/phone) inside list item 4 rather than as a separate page footer element. While this doesn't fabricate content, it structurally misattributes the footer content as part of the list item content. No fabricated text was found.
findings.evidence: The footer text 'North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality | Division of Energy...' appears inside the <li> for item 4, matching PDF page 1 footer text exactly.

findings.description: All sections and content from the 4-page PDF are present in the HTML. No sections are missing.
findings.evidence: All 5 numbered items, sub-items A-G under item 5, basin-specific comments, closing paragraphs, signature block, and cc line are all present.
